comments.body: Done (commit pending hash). write_front_matter + _split_front_matter_raw in prompts.py; PROMPT_SCHEMA_VERSION + closed FRONT_MATTER_KEYS; read-side PromptVersionError refuse; .gitattributes eol=lf pins. 10 golden/coexistence tests + full unit tier (1869) green.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

All five Acceptance Criteria are met. Evidence summary:

**AC1 — Canonical writer is idempotent, preserves body byte-for-byte, warns+preserves unknown keys, rejects BOM.**
- `write_front_matter` + `_split_front_matter_raw` in `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` (lines 204–252): canonical key order, `schema_version` stamped, WARN+PRESERVE for unknown keys (appended sorted), BOM rejected for both body and file input, body appended byte-for-byte after closing fence, LF-normalized YAML block with single trailing newline.
- Tests: `test_writer_rejects_bom_body`, `test_raw_split_rejects_bom_file`, `test_unknown_keys_warn_and_are_preserved_sorted`, `test_writer_stamps_schema_version_and_is_idempotent` all in `tests/unit/test_prompt_front_matter.py`. ✅

**AC2 — Golden round-trip tests over fixtures including the three boundary corruptors (port spike E1).**
- `test_body_preserved_byte_for_byte` is parametrized with all three required corruptors: (1) no-trailing-NL, (2) embedded CRLF in body, (3) body that itself starts with `---`, plus empty and normal cases. Each verifies `write_front_matter` and `_split_front_matter_raw` round-trip losslessly. ✅

**AC3 — A higher `schema_version` than the binary triggers graceful warn+skip (read-side), tested.**
- `_refuse_newer_schema_version` (prompts.py:188–201) raises `PromptVersionError` (a `PromptError` subclass) with a clear message. Called from both `parse_front_matter` and `_split_front_matter_raw`.
- `test_higher_schema_version_is_refused_on_read` exercises both read paths. ✅

**AC4 — `.gitattributes` pins prompt/index file globs to `eol=lf`.**
- `.gitattributes` (lines 9–12): `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/*.md eol=lf`, `.rebar/prompts/** eol=lf`, `.rebar/prompts.index.json eol=lf`, `.rebar/workflows/** eol=lf`. ✅

**AC5 — Write-side version policy documented: bump ONLY when emitting a key a prior version lacked; deploy order is readers-before-writers.**
- Documented in `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` lines 124–126: "deploy order is readers-before-writers, like TAG_DELTA. The WRITER bumps this only when it begins emitting a key a prior version lacked." Also referenced in the `_refuse_newer_schema_version` docstring. ✅

Note: The `verify_commands` reference `tests/unit/test_frontmatter_roundtrip.py` but the actual file is `tests/unit/test_prompt_front_matter.py` — all required test coverage is present under that name.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The contract HOME and the safe authoring substrate. Front-matter carries each prompt's contract;
the writer must round-trip without corruption (item-4 requirement), validated by spike E1.

## What
- Define the CLOSED front-matter key set (Prompty spellings): `schema_version, title, description,
  inputs, outputs, execution_mode, category, model, tags, dimension, applies_to, langfuse_prompt,
  default`. Heavy JSON Schemas referenced BY NAME (not inlined). `inputs`/`outputs` are the prompt's
  contract surface (see the epic Terminology note).
- Build the parse-split-rejoin canonical writer over PyYAML in `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py` (extend
  `parse_front_matter`; add `write_front_matter`): split on `---`, canonical key order, unknown-key
  WARN+PRESERVE (appended deterministically), LF + single trailing newline, no BOM, body byte-for-byte,
  idempotent (`wb(wb(x))==wb(x)`). Stamp `schema_version`.
- READ-SIDE version coexistence: when a file's `schema_version` is HIGHER than the running binary
  understands, the reader emits a clear WARNING and skips/refuses THAT prompt gracefully (never renders
  unknown front-matter into the body) — mixed-fleet safety, upgrade-reconcile-hosts-first (like TAG_DELTA).
- Ship `.gitattributes` pinning prompt/index files to `eol=lf`.

## Scope
In: format + reader/writer + version-coexistence + golden tests. Out: migrating the reviewers (S3), resolver (S3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical writer is idempotent, preserves the body byte-for-byte (incl. no-trailing-NL / CRLF / body-starting-with---), warns+preserves unknown keys, rejects BOM.
- [ ] Golden round-trip tests over fixtures including the three boundary corruptors (port spike E1).
- [ ] A file with a higher `schema_version` than the binary triggers a graceful warn+skip (read-side), tested.
- [ ] `.gitattributes` pins the prompt/index file globs to `eol=lf`.
- [ ] WRITE-side version policy: a writer bumps `schema_version` ONLY when it emits a key the prior version lacked; the documented deploy order is readers-before-writers (the read-side refuse is the safety net, not the primary gate).
compiled_state.title: Prompt front-matter format + canonical writer (parse-split-rejoin over PyYAML)
